purpose: Build layered research memory for Case 2: vague vitality question -> subjective streetscape perception -> nonlinear and spatial heterogeneity workflow.
processing_rule: Run MinerU one paper at a time. Generate a single-paper input JSON for each paper_id and inspect output before moving on.

status_legend.memory_extracted: layered memory note has been written
status_legend.failed: processing failed and needs repair or replacement

papers.title: Beyond objective features: Integrating subjective perceptions to decipher urban vitality via multimodal machine learning
papers.pdfPath: D:/论文库/论文库/Beyond objective features_ Integrating subjective perceptions to decipher urban vitality via multimodal machine learning.pdf
papers.why_selected: Directly connects subjective perception, objective built environment, urban vitality, and multimodal machine learning.

papers.title: Using street view images to examine the association between human perceptions of locale and urban vitality in Shenzhen, China
papers.pdfPath: D:/论文库/论文库/Using street view images to examine the association between human perceptions of locale and urban vitality in Shenzhen, China.pdf
papers.why_selected: A close empirical analogue for relating human perception from street-view imagery to urban vitality.

papers.title: Investigating nonlinear and spatially heterogeneous impacts of the built environment on urban vitality
papers.pdfPath: D:/论文库/论文库/Investigating nonlinear and spatially heterogeneous impacts of the built environment on urban vitality.pdf
papers.why_selected: Matches the nonlinear and spatial heterogeneity part of the Case 2 research trajectory.

papers.title: Nonlinear effects of the built environment on urban vitality in Jinan based on multi-source data and explainable AI
papers.pdfPath: D:/论文库/论文库/Nonlinear effects of the built environment on urban vitality in Jinan based on multi-source data and explainable AI.pdf
papers.why_selected: Useful for deciding when interpretable machine learning is justified and how to avoid causal overclaiming.

papers.title: Exploring the association between street built environment and street vitality using deep learning methods
papers.pdfPath: D:/论文库/论文库/Exploring the association between street built environment and street vitality using deep learning methods.pdf
papers.why_selected: Provides a street-scale workflow for extracting visual built-environment features and aligning them with vitality.

papers.title: Examining the association between the built environment and pedestrian volume using street view images
papers.pdfPath: D:/论文库/论文库/Examining the association between the built environment and pedestrian volume using street view images.pdf
papers.why_selected: Helps distinguish observed pedestrian outcome Y from perceptual or POI proxies.

papers.title: Measuring human perceptions of a large-scale urban region using machine learning
papers.pdfPath: D:/论文库/论文库/Measuring human perceptions of a large-scale urban region using machine learning.pdf
papers.why_selected: Supports the question of how subjective built-environment perception can be measured from large-scale imagery.

papers.title: Measuring human perceptions of streetscapes to better inform urban renewal: A perspective of scene semantic parsing
papers.pdfPath: D:/论文库/论文库/Measuring human perceptions of streetscapes to better inform urban renewal_ A perspective of scene semantic parsing.pdf
papers.why_selected: Useful for decomposing subjective perception into visual semantics and interpretable urban-design variables.

papers.title: Beyond sentiment: Using large language models to decode multidimensional urban park perceptions for enhanced equality
papers.pdfPath: D:/论文库/论文库/Beyond sentiment_ Using large language models to decode multidimensional urban park perceptions for enhanced equality.pdf
papers.why_selected: Supports the later natural question about whether large models can evaluate subjective built-environment perception.

papers.title: Multiscale geographical random forest: A novel spatial ML approach for traffic safety modeling integrating street-view semantic visual features
papers.pdfPath: D:/论文库/论文库/Multiscale geographical random forest_ A novel spatial ML approach for traffic safety modeling integrating street-view semantic visual features.pdf
papers.why_selected: Provides a spatial machine-learning reference for when local heterogeneity methods are appropriate.
